title: Speedrunning A Random Item
description: Challenge yourself to obtain a randomly selected item as fast as possible — with twists. Roll complications like No Sprinting, Meat Only, Inverted Mouse, or No Armor and race agains
body:
#  Speedrunning A Random Item

A singleplayer challenge mod for Minecraft 1.21.11.  
Roll a random target item, pick a complication, and get it as fast as you can.
This mod almost completely repeats the rubric of the YouTuber KompotikIvun

## How it works

When you create a world, a new **Random** tab appears in the world creation screen.  
Roll a random item and a random complication, then start the world.  
The timer starts on your first input — mouse movement or keypress.  
Collect the required number of the target item to stop the clock.

## Complications

27 complications that make the run harder (or just weirder):

| Category | Complications |
|---|---|
| **Movement** | Snail (no sprinting), Giant (×2 size), Midget (×0.5 size) |
| **Vision** | Mole (min render distance), Noir (black & white shader), Cockeyed (inverted mouse) |
| **Diet** | Vegetarian (no meat), Carnivore (meat only) |
| **Inventory** | Holes in Pockets (hotbar only), One-armed (no offhand), Fragile Bones (no armor) |
| **World** | Vastness (Large Biomes), Maximalism (Amplified) |
| **Danger** | Hydrophobe (instant death in water), Thalassophobe (pushed away from oceans) |
| **Sensitivity** | Turtle (min mouse sensitivity), Sonic (max mouse sensitivity) |
| **Social** | Antisocial (villagers won't trade), Insomnia (can't sleep) |
| **Perspective** | Protagonist (first person only), NPC (third person only) |
| **Misc** | Deafness (no sound), Acceleration (×2 tickrate), Ignoramus (no recipe book), Twins (collect ×2), Triplets (collect ×3) |

## Features

- **In-game HUD** — shows target item, active complication, and live timer
- **Death screen shortcuts** — restart with the same seed or roll a new one instantly
- **Manual selection** — don't want a random pick? Browse and choose any item or complication manually
- **Persistent timer** — leaving and rejoining the world doesn't reset your time
- **Attempt tracking** — worlds are automatically named with attempt numbers on restart
